Dashboard Archive: 2026-05-15 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum ranker shows NBR commanding the top position with an exceptional composite score of 96.9, driven by extraordinary long-term returns of 224.51% over one year while maintaining pristine technical structure across all four major moving averages. NBR's ratio consistency scores nearly perfect at 97.97, indicating gains have compounded smoothly across timeframes from daily through yearly, and it demonstrates remarkable resilience with a 99.04 bad-spy-day score, suggesting it outperforms during market stress. PUMP ranks second with a 188.43% one-year return but shows a weaker bad-spy-day score of 4.62 compared to NBR's 2.87, indicating less stability during broader market downturns despite passing all SMA gates and maintaining 4 out of 5 ratio consistency checks. Both tickers show strong correlation to energy sector exposure (XLE at 0.54 and 0.528 respectively), suggesting their momentum may be driven by similar market factors rather than independent strength. The data reflects a market environment where select high-momentum names with structural integrity are being rewarded, but the concentration of these returns in energy-correlated assets warrants attention to sector concentration risks.
